title: GDPR Data Retention Policy Enforcement

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native `| rest` API, no separate TA required)
spl:
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*", "history", "summary")
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400, 1)
| eval policy_max_days=case(
    match(title,"^(hr|pii|gdpr)"), 180,
    match(title,"^(security|sec)"), 365,
    1=1, 365)
| eval violation=if(retention_days>policy_max_days, "Exceeds_Policy", "OK")
| table title, retention_days, policy_max_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, violation
| sort - retention_days
description: Audits Splunk index-level retention settings against written data retention policy so personal data in logs is not kept longer than necessary under the storage limitation principle.
value: Audits Splunk index-level retention settings against written data retention policy so personal data in logs is not kept longer than necessary under the storage limitation principle.
implementation: (1) Run from a scheduled search on the search head (requires admin capability for REST); (2) replace the `case()` block with a lookup `index_retention_policy.csv` mapping index names to required max retention days; (3) export results to GRC tickets when violations trigger; (4) pair with archive/freeze path review outside Splunk for complete retention evidence.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native `| rest` API, no separate TA required).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: REST endpoint: `/services/data/indexes` ‚Äî fields: `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `maxTotalDataSizeMB`, `disabled`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Run from a scheduled search on the search head (requires admin capability for REST); (2) replace the `case()` block with a lookup `index_retention_policy.csv` mapping index names to required max retention days; (3) export results to GRC tickets when violations trigger; (4) pair with archive/freeze path review outside Splunk for complete retention evidence.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*", "history", "summary")
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400, 1)
| eval policy_max_days=case(
    match(title,"^(hr|pii|gdpr)"), 180,
    match(title,"^(security|sec)"), 365,
    1=1, 365)
| eval violation=if(retention_days>policy_max_days, "Exceeds_Policy", "OK")
| table title, retention_days, policy_max_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, violation
| sort - retention_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Data Retention Policy Enforcement (Art. 5(1)(e))** ‚Äî Audits Splunk index-level retention settings against written data retention policy so personal data in logs is not kept longer than necessary under the storage limitation principle.

Documented **Data sources**: REST endpoint: `/services/data/indexes` ‚Äî fields: `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `maxTotalDataSizeMB`, `disabled`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native `| rest` API, no separate TA required). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **retention_days** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **policy_max_days** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **violation** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **GDPR Data Retention Policy Enforcement (Art. 5(1)(e))**): table title, retention_days, policy_max_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, violation
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (index, retention, policy, violation), Bar chart (retention by index), Single value (violation count).
visualization: Table (index, retention, policy, violation), Bar chart (retention by index), Single value (violation count).
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands ‚Äî correlate with change management windows.
